title: sumacus copallina
intro: Sumacus copallina, also known as the Stag's Horn Sumac, is a shrub native to North America, characterized by its distinctive red berries and edible leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sumacus copallina include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a natural remedy for various skin conditions. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to reap its benefits.
